message:
feat(namepool): Add themed name pools for polecats

Polecats now get themed names from the Mad Max universe by default
(furiosa, nux, slit, etc.) instead of generic polecat-01, polecat-02.

Changes:
- Add NamepoolConfig to config/types.go for per-rig theme configuration
- Update namepool.go with three built-in themes:
  - mad-max (default): furiosa, nux, imperator, etc.
  - minerals: obsidian, quartz, ruby, etc.
  - wasteland: rust, chrome, fury, etc.
- Add gt namepool commands: themes, set, add, reset
- Update manager.go to load namepool config from rig settings

Configuration in .gastown/config.json:
```json
{
  "namepool": {
    "style": "minerals",
    "max_before_numbering": 50
  }
}
```

Issue: beads-rs0

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"gt-0pc","title":"Document Overseer role (human operator)","description":"Document the Overseer role in Gas Town architecture.

## The Overseer

The **Overseer** is the human operator of Gas Town. Not an agent - a person.

## Responsibilities

| Area | Overseer Does | Mayor/Agents Do |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| Strategy | Define project goals | Execute toward goals |
| Priorities | Set priority order | Work in priority order |
| Escalations | Final decision on stuck work | Escalate to Overseer |
| Resources | Provision machines | Use allocated resources |
| Quality | Review & approve swarm output | Produce output |
| Operations | Run gt commands, monitor dashboards | Do the work |

## Key Interactions

### Overseer → Mayor
- Start/stop Mayor sessions
- Direct Mayor via conversation
- Review Mayor recommendations
- Approve cross-rig decisions

### Mayor → Overseer (Escalations)
- Stuck workers after retries
- Resource decisions (add machines, polecats)
- Ambiguous requirements
- Architecture decisions

## Operating Cadence

Typical Overseer workflow:
1. Morning: Check status, review overnight work
2. During day: Monitor, respond to escalations, adjust priorities
3. End of day: Review progress, plan next batch

## Commands for Overseers

```bash
gt status              # Quick health check
gt doctor              # Detailed diagnostics  
gt doctor --fix        # Auto-repair issues
gt inbox               # Messages from agents
gt stop --all          # Emergency halt
```

## Documentation Updates

Add to docs/architecture.md:
- Overseer section under Agent Roles
- Clarify Mayor reports to Overseer
- Add Overseer to workflow diagrams","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:03.177633-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:51.477786-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:51.477786-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-17r","title":"Doctor check: Zombie session cleanup","description":"Detect and clean up zombie tmux sessions via gt doctor.

## Problem

Zombie sessions occur when:
- Agent crashes without cleanup
- gt kill fails mid-operation
- System restart leaves orphan sessions
- Session naming collision

## Checks

### ZombieSessionCheck
- List all tmux sessions matching gt-* pattern
- Cross-reference with known polecats
- Flag sessions with no corresponding polecat state
- Flag sessions for removed polecats
- Check session age vs polecat creation time

### Detection Criteria
- Session exists but polecat directory doesn't
- Session name doesn't match any registered polecat
- Polecat state=idle but session running
- Multiple sessions for same polecat

## Output

```
[WARN] Zombie tmux sessions detected:
  - gt-wyvern-OldPolecat (polecat removed)
  - gt-beads-Unknown (no matching polecat)
  - gt-wyvern-Toast (duplicate session)

  Run 'gt doctor --fix' to clean up
```

## Auto-Fix (--fix flag)

- Kill orphan tmux sessions
- Update polecat state to match reality
- Log all cleanup actions

## Safety

- Never kill sessions where polecat state=working
- Prompt before killing if --fix used without --force
- Create audit log of killed sessions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:01.446702-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:39.517644-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-17r","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:05.66301-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-17r","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:44:41.945064-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-ebl","title":"CLI: names commands for polecat naming pool","description":"Polecat naming pool for auto-generated names.

## Commands

### gt names generate
```
gt names generate [--count N]
```
Generate N names from pool.

### gt names add
```
gt names add <name>...
```
Add custom names to pool.

### gt names list
```
gt names list
```
Show available and used names.

### gt names reset
```
gt names reset [--keep-used]
```
Reset pool to defaults.

## Config File
<rig>/town/naming.json:
```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "auto_refill": true,
  "refill_threshold": 5,
  "pool": {
    "available": ["Toast", "Nux", "Capable", ...],
    "used": ["Alice", "Bob"]
  }
}
```

## Default Pool
Mad Max themed: Toast, Nux, Capable, Furiosa, Immortan, etc.

## Integration
gt polecat add calls naming pool if no name given:
```go
if name == "" {
    name, err = naming.Generate(rigPath)
}
```

## New Package
internal/naming/
├── pool.go     # Pool management
└── defaults.go # Default name lists

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Auto-generate names on polecat add
- [ ] Track used vs available
- [ ] Auto-refill when low
- [ ] Custom names addable","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:33.592129-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:07:13.882465-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-eu9","title":"Witness session cycling and handoff","description":"Add session cycling and handoff protocol to Witness CLAUDE.md template.

## Session Cycling Protocol

```markdown
## Session Cycling

Your context will fill over long swarms. Proactively cycle when:
- Running for many hours
- Losing track of which workers you've checked
- Responses getting slower
- About to start complex operation

### Handoff Protocol

1. **Capture current state**:
```bash
town list .           # Worker states
town all beads        # Pending verifications  
town inbox            # Unprocessed messages
```

2. **Compose handoff note**:
```
[HANDOFF_TYPE]: witness_cycle
[TIMESTAMP]: <now>
[RIG]: <rig>

## Active Workers
<list workers and status>

## Pending Verifications
<workers signaled done but not verified>

## Recent Actions
<last 3-5 actions>

## Warnings/Notes
<anything next session should know>

## Next Steps
<what should happen next>
```

3. **Send handoff**:
```bash
town mail send <rig>/witness -s "Session Handoff" -m "<note>"
```

4. **Exit cleanly**: End session, daemon spawns fresh one.

### On Fresh Session Start

1. Check for handoff: `town inbox | grep "Session Handoff"`
2. If found, read it and resume from handoff state
3. If not found, do full status check
```

## Implementation

Add to WITNESS_CLAUDE.md template.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:48:55.484911-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:47:30.768506-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-eu9","depends_on_id":"gt-82y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:49:05.846443-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-f8v","title":"Witness pre-kill verification protocol","description":"Add pre-kill verification protocol to Witness CLAUDE.md template.

## Protocol for Witness Prompting

```markdown
## Pre-Kill Verification Protocol

Before killing any worker session, verify workspace is clean.

### Verification Steps

When a worker signals done:

1. **Capture worker state**:
```bash
town capture <polecat> "git status && git stash list && bd sync --status"
```

2. **Assess the output** (use your judgment):
- Is working tree clean?
- Is stash list empty?
- Is beads synced?

3. **Decision**:
- **CLEAN**: Proceed to kill session
- **DIRTY**: Send nudge with specific issues

### Nudge Templates

**Uncommitted Changes**:
```
town inject <polecat> "WITNESS CHECK: Uncommitted changes found. Please commit or discard: <files>. Signal done when clean."
```

**Beads Not Synced**:
```
town inject <polecat> "WITNESS CHECK: Beads not synced. Run 'bd sync' then commit. Signal done when complete."
```

### Kill Sequence

Only after verification passes:
```bash
town kill <polecat>
town sleep <polecat>
```

### Escalation

If worker fails verification 3+ times:
```bash
town mail send mayor/ -s "Escalation: <polecat> stuck" -m "Cannot complete cleanup after 3 attempts. Issues: <list>."
```
```

## Implementation

-	// Initialize name pool
-	pool := NewNamePool(r.Path, r.Name)
+	// Try to load rig config for namepool settings
+	rigConfigPath := filepath.Join(r.Path, ".gastown", "config.json")
+	var pool *NamePool
+
+	rigConfig, err := config.LoadRigConfig(rigConfigPath)
+	if err == nil && rigConfig.Namepool != nil {
+		// Use configured namepool settings
+		pool = NewNamePoolWithConfig(
+			r.Path,
+			r.Name,
+			rigConfig.Namepool.Style,
+			rigConfig.Namepool.Names,
+			rigConfig.Namepool.MaxBeforeNumbering,
+		)
+	} else {
+		// Use defaults
+		pool = NewNamePool(r.Path, r.Name)
+	}
 	_ = pool.Load() // Load existing state, ignore errors for new rigs
 
 	return &Manager{
